study_group_representative.quote: As a low-middle income and populous country with 95 million people, Vietnam faces a high risk of a severe COVID-19 outbreak... Vietnam has kept the outbreak under control since the first cases were confirmed more than 2 months ago.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: Vietnam’s responses are generally in line with current practices of other countries, but they were implemented very early, with great emphasis on case identification, source control and public awareness... As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: The first phase lasted until 5 March, characterized by case importation from Asian countries... On 23 March, the first case without an epidemiological link was reported... As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality.

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: The government has kept the public constantly updated with the outbreak progression and responses via accessible official channels...

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: As of 7 April, Vietnam recorded 241 confirmed cases and no fatality. The country has kept the outbreak at a slow growth rate even after reporting 100 cases, with doubling time of more than 7 days, on par with Japan or Singapore (Figure 2, bottom panel).
